name: Muslim Association of Puget Sound

theoryOfChange: Investing in education (scholarships/seminaries) and social services (clinics/assistance) to produce leaders and a stable community that follows the prophetic example of service.
scoreSummary: Muslim Association of Puget Sound shows strong impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $7,286,362 and a program expense ratio of 81.7%.
The PNW Seminary provides structured programs for Islamic scholarship and leadership.
MAPS tracks outcomes including food/gas card distribution and small business outreach.
The organization mobilizes funding for small businesses and serves thousands of students.
The organization manages high attendance for Friday congregational prayers.
MAPS provides essential services including housing for women and medical clinic access.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and provides multiple giving methods for donors.
The organization holds 17.3 months of working capital and a fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 70/100 score due to its robust financial health and high alignment with donor needs, though the score is tempered by medium data confidence. It demonstrates a strong ability to convert revenue into direct community impact through religious and social services.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing data transparency to improve the overall confidence score from its current medium level.
Expanding direct beneficiary reporting across all specialized educational programs to further validate cost-per-beneficiary metrics.

dimension_explanations.alignment: MAPS shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a Zakat-eligible institution that addresses urgent religious and cultural needs through programs like the PNW Seminary and daily prayers [2][7]. Its long track record is evidenced by consistent annual reporting on community metrics including Iftar meals served and small business support [3][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization maintains a direct service model with a program expense ratio of 81.7%, focusing on tangible outcomes such as housing women in permanent shelters and providing medical clinic services [1][6]. While the cost per beneficiary is calculated at approximately $778.64, the impact is bolstered by high operational efficiency and significant working capital of 17.3 months [1][9].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound serves as a vital community hub providing comprehensive religious, educational, and social services to the Pacific Northwest.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 81.7%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support community services.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.02 to raise every $1.
Strong liquidity with 17.3 months of working capital available to sustain operations.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound (MAPS) operates as a multi-faceted religious organization, managing a total revenue of $7,286,362 to support its diverse community initiatives [1]. Its core offerings include the PNW Seminary for Islamic scholarship, daily congregational prayers, and extensive educational programming for adults and youth [2][5].

alignment.explanation: MAPS is a high-fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="10">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serving asnaf categories including <cite id="18">the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin)</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The organization could further improve alignment by <cite id="19">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust MAPS due to its <cite id="9">100% independent board</cite> and long-standing presence since <cite id="1">2006</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="16">expand its board size</cite> beyond the current four members to meet higher governance benchmarks.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly efficient with an <cite id="4">81.7% program ratio</cite> and a direct service model that <cite id="7">prevents evictions</cite> and <cite id="12">educates thousands</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="17">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> to better articulate the long-term outcomes of its diverse programs.

rich_narrative.headline: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound serves as a vital community hub providing comprehensive religious, educational, and social services to the Pacific Northwest.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support a thriving local Muslim ecosystem that combines religious scholarship with robust social safety nets.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through a locally-rooted institution
Value high-quality Islamic education and seminary programs
Prioritize organizations with extremely low fundraising overhead
Support holistic community hubs that provide both spiritual and material aid
giving_considerations: Donations support a regional hub rather than international relief
Strong focus on the Pacific Northwest Muslim community
Financial stability is high, making it a safe choice for recurring giving
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international emergency relief or those who prefer organizations with large, multi-national boards.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcome tracking across multiple years, including specific metrics for meals, housing, and education.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MAPS creates impact by providing spiritual guidance, educational pathways, and social services to strengthen community resilience.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking specific outcomes like eviction prevention ensures that social service funds are meeting the most urgent community needs.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 20.8% growth rate and expanding programs like the PNW Seminary, MAPS has significant capacity to absorb new capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by <cite id="5">superior fundraising efficiency</cite> compared to the <cite id="20">peer program ratio median of 89.6%</cite>.

strengths.detail: MAPS directs <cite id="4">81.7% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs, exceeding the industry standard of 75%.
The organization spends only <cite id="5">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, maximizing the impact of every dollar donated.
The charity maintains <cite id="11">17.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant stability for long-term operations.

strengths_deep_dive: MAPS exhibits a high degree of operational maturity and community impact through its multi-pronged service model. Their educational reach is substantial, with <cite id="12">3,000 total enrollments</cite> across various programs and <cite id="13">100 registrations for the PNW Seminary</cite> specifically. Beyond education, their humanitarian efforts are quantifiable and significant, serving <cite id="14">20,500 Iftar meals</cite> in a single year. This combination of religious infrastructure and direct social service delivery is supported by a <cite id="15">3-year revenue CAGR of 20.8%</cite>, indicating strong community trust and sustainable growth.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound (MAPS) serves as a cornerstone of the Pacific Northwest Muslim community, operating with a mission to provide religious, educational, and social services. Founded in <cite id="1">2006</cite>, the organization has grown into a significant regional institution with a <cite id="2">total revenue of $7,286,362</cite>. MAPS manages a diverse portfolio of programs including the <cite id="3">PNW Seminary for Islamic scholarship</cite>, daily congregational prayers, and the MAPS-MCRC social services arm. The organization demonstrates strong financial stewardship, maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 81.7%</cite>, which ensures that the vast majority of donor funds are directed toward community services rather than administration. Their operational efficiency is further highlighted by a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>, significantly outperforming many peer organizations. In addition to religious services, MAPS provides critical humanitarian aid; in recent years, they have <cite id="6">distributed 75,000 food and gas cards</cite> and <cite id="7">kept 70 families from eviction</cite>. The organization also fosters economic resilience, having <cite id="8">mobilized $450,000 for small businesses</cite>. With <cite id="9">100% independent board members</cite> and a <cite id="10">verified zakat program</cite>, MAPS offers a transparent and culturally aligned platform for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations while supporting local community development.
